title: Wire or retire Rust Meshtastic config handler
description: Why this exists: Schrodinger review found rust/lichen-meshtastic/src/config_handler.rs is not exported or compiled by rust/lichen-meshtastic/src/lib.rs, so cargo check can pass while stale admin/config behavior diverges from the Meshtastic compatibility contract. What needs to be done: decide whether the Rust handler remains reference/tooling or should be compiled; wire it into lib.rs or remove/replace it; update tests so unsupported admin writes and read-only owner/channel/config/metadata responses match docs/meshtastic-compat-dev.md. Acceptance criteria: cargo check/test covers the handler or it is explicitly removed from the reference surface; stale unsupported-write behavior is not silently present.
notes:
Decision: retire stale Rust config_handler.rs rather than wire it. Evidence: temporary lib.rs export experiment made cargo check -p lichen-meshtastic fail with 48 errors because config_handler.rs expected generated AdminMessage/Channel/Config/DeviceMetadata/LoRaConfig fields/types that are not present in the checked-in Rust protobuf subset. Keeping the file unexported would allow stale Admin/config behavior to diverge silently.

Implemented: deleted rust/lichen-meshtastic/src/config_handler.rs. Updated docs/meshtastic-compat-dev.md to state Rust no longer carries a separate Admin/config handler, active read-only config sync is Zephyr-owned via want_config_id staging, and packet-level ADMIN_APP reads are deferred to project-LICHEN-t2hn.23. This avoids claiming currently unsupported Zephyr ADMIN_APP reads are implemented.

Validation: rg -n 'config_handler|ConfigHandler|LichenConfig|handle_admin_request' after deleting rust/target found no tracked references. git diff --check PASS. cargo test -p lichen-meshtastic PASS: 27/27 tests plus doc-tests. cargo test -p lichen-meshtastic --features std PASS: 27/27 tests plus doc-tests. Deleted generated rust/target after tests.

Review outcomes: Boyle the 3rd found P1 doc overstatement that Zephyr owned packet-level Admin/config handling while current Zephyr treats ADMIN_APP as unsupported; fixed by distinguishing want_config_id sync from deferred ADMIN_APP work. Mendel the 3rd found same P2 overstatement and verified config_handler.rs was not compiled/exported; fixed. Kant the 3rd found no P0-P2 findings, verified no remaining references and cargo tests including --features std. Russell the 3rd narrow re-review after doc fix found no P0-P2 findings and confirmed deletion satisfies project-LICHEN-t2hn.20.
close_reason: Retired stale uncompiled Rust Meshtastic config handler. Verified export experiment failed against current schema, deleted the file, documented Zephyr/current sync ownership and deferred ADMIN_APP reads, ran Rust tests, and completed independent reviews.
